titlestmt.titleproper: Cornell University banners, clothing, and other textiles47-7-3439
titlestmt.author: Compiled by RMC Staff

publicationstmt.publisher: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

profiledesc.creation: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2022-09-16 18:55:12 UTC.

change.item: This resource was modified by the ArchivesSpace Preprocessor developed by the Harvard Library (https://github.com/harvard-library/archivesspace-preprocessor)

repository.corpname: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

did.unittitle: Cornell University banners, clothing, and other textiles
did.abstract: Banners, pennants, pillows, runners, and other cloth items.

defitem.label: Contact Information:
defitem.item: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524rareref@cornell.eduhttp://rmc.library.cornell.edu

relatedmaterial.head: RELATED MATERIALS
relatedmaterial.p: See Stephen J. Roberts Papers, #24-2-2903, for an additional banner.
See also Cigarette silk collection, #6612, for additional Cornell University related textiles.

prefercite.head: Cite As:
prefercite.p: Cornell University Banners, Clothing, and Other Textiles, #47-7-3439. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.

scopecontent.head: Scope and content
scopecontent.p: Banners, pennants, pillows, runners, jackets, blazers, reunion items, graduation robes and regalia, stuffed animal mascots, and other cloth items. Examples include a Cornell University doctoral hood, originally owned by Dr. William J. Edens, Ph.D. 1937 and Class of 1941 blazer owned by Arthur Townsend Klinger.

did.unittitle: Series I: Banners, Pennants, and Flags
Banner – "When Better Women are Made Cornell Men Will Make Them"
Large Cornell flag, probably owned by John Sherwood Hoffmire, Class of 1916
Small banner - "Baker Court, Souvenir of Ithaca"
Cayuga Club Banner, a club that later became a chapter of the Sigma Phi Sigma Fraternity
Red banner with white "'84", class of 1884
Cornell banner, red with white lettering
Cornell banner with "Cornell" written on each end and raised emblem in center
Green banner with "1920s here" in white

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Two identical banners, but MU-2439 has a CU button and football pin in the center

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Shown as part of the Sesquicentennial Exhibition

did.unittitle: Cornell pennant belonging to Dr. Alexander Powell Hyde, Class of 1945
Cornell pennant with printed emblem and letters
Cornell pennant with emblem and red lettering on white
White canvas banner with "1893" in red
White canvas banner with "1896" in red
White canvas banner with "1897" in red
White canvas banner with "1901" in red
White canvas banner with "1902" in red
White canvas banner with "1903" in red
White canvas banner with "1904" in red
White canvas banner with "1905" in red
White canvas banner with "1906" in red
White canvas banner with "1907" in red
White canvas banner with "1908" in red
White canvas banner with "1909" in red
White canvas banner with "1910" in red
White canvas banner with "1911" in red
White canvas banner with "1912" in red
White canvas banner with "1920" in red
White canvas banner with "1923" in red
White canvas banner with "1924" in red
White canvas banner with "1925" in red
White canvas banner with "1926" in red
White canvas banner with "1928" in red
Gold banner with "Cornell '95" in blue letters and vice versa
Red felt banner with "Cornell" in white

did.physdesc: Rectangular red felt banner with yellow and gray logo.

did.unittitle: Series II: Pillows and Decorative Items
Laundry bag given to freshman in 1952
Patchwork quilt by Dilbert H. Decker, class of 1886
Red Wool Felt Pillow with "Cornell" in white
Felt Cornell pillow cover owned by Guy Gundaker
2 pillowcases with red satin trim, embroidered wheel and signatures

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: MU-534: Signatures of men with date of graduation

did.unittitle: Red and white felt pillow cover
Red cloth napkins with Cornell University logo printed on them
Red cloth table runner with Cornell
Hand-embroidered pillow case with the Cornell insignia

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The letter "G" is embroidered on verso.

did.physdesc: Beige pillow with Cornell embroidered in red. Red rope lines the edges with red tassels in upper left corner.

did.unittitle: Straw hat with "Cornell" written on ribbon
PhD hood with gold velvet lining

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes two robes one with Cornell seal embroidered on a field of blue, hoods, and mortar boards. One cap has initials BLR inside. Some of these items were worn while Rideout led President Frank Rhodes' 1977 inauguration procession.

did.unittitle: Kenneth C. Newman, Class of 1910, reunion jacket
Kenneth C. Newman, Class of 1910, reunion hat

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 52" by 36" piece of white-dotted indigo percale, with label depicting Cornell Crew member standing on dock. Manufactured by W.P. Co.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Red silk scarf with "C27" printed in corner.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Red and white vertical stripes with "C27" on chest.

did.unittitle: Ph.D. hood owned by William Edens
Straw hat with "'40" written on it
Class of 1941 blazer owned by Arthur Townsend Klinger
Class of 1957 Freshman Beanie, owned by Carl Schwarz

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Red beanie with no numbering or lettering, likely from the class of 1956.

did.unittitle: Straw hat with "Cornell '60" written on ribbon
Red sesquicentennial scarf created by the Johnson School
Series IV: Patches, Badges, and Tags
Kenneth C. Newman, Class of 1910, white leather square with straps
Series V. Mascots and stuffed animals

did.physdesc: Tan bear wearing red arm band with a white "C" on it.
Red felt dog with white jacket and red "C"
White fleece elephant with red '26 pennant. Both ears are detached.
Small fleece elephant. One ear is detached.
Red and white felt elephant with "Cornell" written on both ears and '26 embroidered on its right side.
Stuffed white dog with painted red fur and "XXX" barrel around neck.
Red bean-bag bear, with Cornell printed on chest and tongue sticking out.
Red and white felt turtle with bonnet.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Yellow and blue stuffed goat with small ribbon around its neck.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Manufactured in Syracuse, New York. Printed Cornell insignia on both ears.
